daily_brief: The Middle East conflict dominated the day's news as Israel carried out a fresh wave of strikes across Iran while Tehran launched missile attacks on Israel and Gulf states, including strikes that damaged Kuwait International Airport [France 24, BBC, Globe and Mail]. Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi flatly rejected US ceasefire proposals and denied any ongoing negotiations, though President Trump insisted talks were continuing [Al Jazeera, The Hindu, Financial Times]. The conflict's economic ripples spread globally, with Brent crude topping $104 per barrel and South Korean tech giants Samsung and SK urging employees to conserve fuel [Al Jazeera, The Hindu]. In a significant development for Ukraine, President Zelensky revealed that Washington is conditioning security guarantees on Kyiv ceding the Donbas region to Russia, citing US preoccupation with the Iran conflict [France 24, The Guardian]. In a landmark US court ruling, Meta and YouTube were found liable for harming a young woman through addictive platform design, with jurors awarding $6 million in damages—a verdict likely to influence thousands of similar pending cases [BBC, Le Monde, Globe and Mail]. The UN General Assembly voted 123-3 to designate the transatlantic slave trade as 'the gravest crime against humanity,' with the US, Israel, and Argentina opposing and 52 nations including the UK abstaining [Guardian, BBC, RFI].

top_stories.headline: Israel Launches Wide-Scale Strikes on Iran as Tehran Rejects US Ceasefire Plan
bullets: Israeli military carried out what it described as 'wide-scale' strikes across Iran on Thursday, while Iranian missiles struck central Israel, wounding two people in Kafr Qassem

Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated Tehran has not engaged in talks with the US and has 'no intention of negotiating,' rejecting a reported 15-point US proposal addressing sanctions relief, nuclear rollback, and reopening the Strait of Hormuz
Iranian attacks also struck Gulf states including Kuwait International Airport, where a fuel tank was hit and caught fire; UAE air defense systems responded to missile threats
President Trump insisted Iran is negotiating despite denials, while the White House warned Iran would be 'hit harder than ever before' if it fails to accept a deal

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Led with Israeli military actions and Trump's 'unleash hell' warning

sources.name: BBC
sources.framing_note: Analytical piece examining what each side wants from potential talks

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Iran's defiance and the details of the rejected US proposal

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Focused on oil price impacts and humanitarian toll on South Asian migrant workers

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Highlighted India being granted Strait of Hormuz passage by Iran as a 'friendly nation'

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Noted 'glimmer of hope' from China regarding talks

top_stories.headline: Zelensky Says US Conditioning Security Guarantees on Ukraine Ceding Donbas to Russia
bullets: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky told Reuters that Washington is making security guarantees conditional on Kyiv surrendering all of the eastern Donbas region to Russia

Zelensky stated the US focus on the Iran conflict is driving Trump to pressure Ukraine for a quick end to the four-year war, saying Trump 'chooses a strategy of putting more pressure on the Ukrainian side'
Zelensky also accused Russia of attempting to blackmail the US by offering to stop sharing military intelligence with Iran in exchange for Washington cutting intelligence sharing with Ukraine
The Ukrainian president warned that ceding Donbas would weaken Ukraine's defenses and embolden Moscow to restart hostilities after any peace deal

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Straightforward reporting of Zelensky's Reuters interview claims

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Included additional detail on Russian intelligence-sharing 'blackmail' allegations

top_stories.headline: Meta and YouTube Found Liable in Landmark Social Media Addiction Trial, Ordered to Pay $6 Million
bullets: A Los Angeles jury found Meta (Instagram, Facebook) and Google (YouTube) liable for harming a 20-year-old woman through addictive platform design, awarding $3 million in compensatory damages and $3 million in punitive damages
Jurors determined both companies were negligent, knew their platforms posed dangers to minors, failed to adequately warn users, and acted with 'malice, oppression, or fraud'
Meta was assigned 70% of responsibility ($4.2 million total), YouTube 30% ($1.8 million); TikTok and Snap settled before trial on undisclosed terms
The verdict follows a separate New Mexico jury finding against Meta this week and could influence over 1,000 similar pending lawsuits; both companies said they would appeal

sources.name: BBC
sources.framing_note: Emphasized the unprecedented nature of the ruling and implications for similar cases

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Framed as 'day of reckoning' for social media, focused on leverage for plaintiffs in pending cases

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Highlighted this as second verdict against Meta in one week, noted company defenses

top_stories.headline: UN General Assembly Votes to Designate Slave Trade as 'Gravest Crime Against Humanity'
bullets: The UN General Assembly passed a resolution 123-3 declaring the transatlantic slave trade 'the gravest crime against humanity' and calling for member states to consider apologies and contributions to a reparations fund
The United States, Israel, and Argentina voted against; 52 countries abstained including the United Kingdom and EU member states

Ghana's President John Mahama, who proposed the resolution, said: 'Let it be recorded that when history beckoned, we did what was right for the memory of millions who suffered the indignity of slavery'

The UK's representative said Britain 'firmly' believes no single atrocity should be regarded as more significant than another; the resolution is not legally binding but carries weight of global opinion

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Emphasized reparatory justice angle and UK parliamentary petition for state apology

sources.name: BBC
sources.framing_note: Balanced coverage noting both supporters' framing as historic and abstaining nations' positions

sources.name: RFI
sources.framing_note: Focused on African nations' strong backing and Mahama's advocacy

top_stories.headline: Hungary's Foreign Minister Accused of Sharing EU Council Discussions with Russia in Real Time
bullets: The Washington Post reported that Hungarian Foreign Minister Péter Szijjártó has been informing Russian counterpart Sergey Lavrov in real time about EU Foreign Affairs Council discussions

Germany's Frankfurter Allgemeine Zeitung confirmed regular exchanges between the Hungarian and Russian ministers, citing former Lithuanian Foreign Minister Gabrielius Landsbergis who was aware of the situation since 2024

EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas's spokesperson reminded member states of their 'legal duty of sincere cooperation'; Germany's Foreign Ministry stated it 'will not tolerate' any breach of confidentiality
The controversy erupts amid Hungary's election campaign and ongoing tensions between Brussels and Budapest over Hungary's ties to Moscow

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Framed as scandal causing alarm in Brussels over possible transfer of sensitive information to Moscow

additional_stories.headline: Ousted Venezuelan President Maduro to appear in New York court for second time since January capture by US forces
Belarus's Lukashenko signs 'friendship and cooperation' treaty with North Korea's Kim Jong Un during first visit to Pyongyang
WHO chief calls Cuba's health crisis 'deeply concerning' as US fuel blockade deepens energy shortages affecting hospitals
French court sentences Swiss Islamic scholar Tariq Ramadan to 18 years in prison for rape of three women
WTO ministerial conference opens in Cameroon amid global trade tensions, US tariffs, and calls for reform
Four Syrian nationals arrested in Germany in operation targeting Channel small boat people-smuggling network
Ukrainian drones strike Estonian power plant chimney and crash in Latvia after veering off course during attack on Russian port
India among five nations granted passage through Strait of Hormuz by Iran alongside China, Russia, Iraq, and Pakistan
Samsung and SK Group urge South Korean employees to reduce car use as government implements emergency energy conservation measures
Danish PM Frederiksen's Social Democrats win election but suffer worst result since 1903, coalition fails to secure majority
